code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Templates now include the subject placeholder so each clause is a full statement.
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "{subject} serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subject} is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} is {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "{subject} has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "{subject} has a runway named {obj}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{subject} is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{subject}'s ICAO identifier is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{subject}'s location identifier is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{subject}'s IATA identifier is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject}'s first runway is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject}'s first runway is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s first runway has a {obj} surface",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{subject}'s first runway number is {obj}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s second runway has a {obj} surface",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s third runway has a {obj} surface",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s fourth runway has a {obj} surface",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s fifth runway has a {obj} surface",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "{subject}'s runway surface is {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "The leader of {subject} is {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subject} is {obj}",
        "language": "{obj} is spoken in {subject}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "currency": "The currency of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "{subject} was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subject}'s postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "{subject}'s area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "{subject}'s largest city is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "{subject}'s county seat is {obj}",
        "mayor": "The mayor of {subject} is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{obj} is the leading party of {subject}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject}'s leader title is {obj}",
        "chief": "{subject}'s chief is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "{subject}'s hub airport is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves the region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "{subject}'s native name is {obj}",
        "jurisdiction": "{subject} has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "{subject} belongs to the class {obj}",
        "division": "{subject} belongs to the division {obj}",
        "order": "{subject} belongs to the order {obj}",
        "species": "{subject} belongs to the species {obj}",
        "demonym": "The demonym of {subject} is {obj}",
    }

    # Helper to format numbers nicely (remove trailing .0 and add commas)
    def _fmt_number(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            num = float(val.replace(",", ""))
            if num.is_integer():
                num = int(num)
            return f"{num:,}"
        except Exception:
            return val

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []  # will store tuples (predicate, phrase, raw_obj)
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            if tmpl:
                parts.append((t.predicate, tmpl.format(obj=obj), obj))
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append((t.predicate, f"{pred} {obj}", obj))

        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                pred, phrase, raw_obj = parts[0]
                # Special handling for a few common predicates
                if pred == "runwayLength":
                    sentence = f"The runway length at {subject} is {raw_obj} meters."
                elif pred == "1stRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence = f"The first runway at {subject} is made from {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "2ndRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence = f"The second runway at {subject} has a {raw_obj.lower()} surface."
                elif pred == "3rdRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence = f"The third runway at {subject} has a {raw_obj.lower()} surface."
                elif pred == "4thRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence = f"The fourth runway at {subject} has a {raw_obj.lower()} surface."
                elif pred == "5thRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence = f"The fifth runway at {subject} has a {raw_obj.lower()} surface."
                elif pred == "locationIdentifier":
                    sentence = f"The location identifier for {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "icaoLocationIdentifier":
                    sentence = f"The ICAO code for {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "iataLocationIdentifier":
                    sentence = f"The IATA code for {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "capital":
                    sentence = f"The capital of {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "language":
                    lang = raw_obj
                    if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                        lang = lang[: -len(" language")]
                    sentence = f"{lang} is spoken in {subject}."
                elif pred == "officialLanguage":
                    lang = raw_obj
                    if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                        lang = lang[: -len(" language")]
                    sentence = f"The official language of {subject} is {lang}."
                elif pred == "demonym":
                    sentence = f"The people of {subject} are called {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "leader":
                    sentence = f"The leader of {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "mayor":
                    sentence = f"The mayor of {subject} is {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "currency":
                    sentence = f"The currency of {subject} is the {raw_obj}."
                elif pred == "country":
                    article = "the " if raw_obj.startswith("United") else ""
                    sentence = f"{subject} is in {article}{raw_obj}."
                elif pred in ("language", "officialLanguage"):
                    lang = raw_obj
                    if lang.lower().endswith(" language"):
                        lang = lang[: -len(" language")]
                    if pred == "language":
                        sentence = f"{lang} is spoken in {subject}."
                    else:
                        sentence = f"{lang} is an official language in {subject}."
                else:
                    sentence = f"{subject} {phrase}."
            else:
                # For multiple predicates we keep the original style
                phrase_list = [p for _, p, _ in parts]
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(phrase_list[:-1]) + ", and " + phrase_list[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
